subject: [PATCH v5 3/3] x86/tdx: Fix zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the x86/tdx code that handles 32-bit port I/O operations. The current implementation only masks the lower 32 bits of the result, preserving the upper 32 bits of RAX. However, according to x86 architecture rules, 32-bit operations should zero-extend the result to 64 bits. The patch uses insn_assign_reg() to write the result back into RAX with proper partial-register-write semantics, ensuring that the correct value is stored in the register. This fix addresses a problem introduced by a previous patch and has been reviewed by multiple developers.
